params.name: Pillars of Creation
params.description:
On Apr 1, 1995 AD, the Hubble Telescope took this famous picture.
The Pillars of Creation is one of the most stunning images captured by the Hubble Telescope. It shows pillars made of interstellar gas and dust inside the Eagle Nebula, located about 7,000 light-years from Earth. This pillar is called the Pillars of Creation because new stars are forming within it. Scientists believe that the pillars were destroyed 6,000 years ago, but we won't see its destruction for another thousand years due …
